Lily of the Valley

Lily of the valley is a plant with white bell shaped flowers hanging from the sides of the stem.

Other names may include:

  • May bells
  • Our lady's tears
  • Mary's tears
  • Apollinaris
The lily of the valley is a perennial in the asparagus family native to Eurasia. This flower specifically is highly poisonous if eaten by animals, including humans. This is due to the extremely high amount of cardiac glycosides throughout the entire plant (including the stem and roots), which can cause severe stomach pain, vomiting, diarrhea and heart arrhythmias which can be fatal if left untreated.

The 3 most popular perennial flowers are:
  1. Geraniums
  2. Petunias
  3. Pansies
